event: positive rapid test for Group A Streptococcus
respiratory rate of 24 breaths per minute
mottling and cyanosis of cheeks, chest, and neck
portable chest radiograph showed right upper lobe infiltrate
no jugular venous distention after fluid resuscitation
pericardial effusion with diastolic collapse of right atrium
decision to proceed with bedside pericardiocentesis
200 milliliters of bloody pericardial fluid drained
open pericardiotomy and pericardial window placement
repeat chest radiograph showed smaller cardiac silhouette
repeat electrocardiogram showed increased voltage of QRS complexes
CT scan showed pericardial drainage tube well positioned
pericardial biopsy and pericardial effusion fluid revealed adenocarcinoma
immune stain suggested pulmonary primary source
bronchial brushing and washing suggested adenocarcinoma
blood, urine, and bronchial-alveolar washing cultures negative
discharged to follow up as an outpatient at the oncology clinic
